bottleneck: compute-bound (partial) + memory-bandwidth (attention QKV projection, no flash attention)

bottleneck_breakdown.compute: attention mm + MLP is dominant; eager attention has O(T^2) memory reads that hit HBM bandwidth, not pure compute
bottleneck_breakdown.memory_bandwidth: eager sdpa reads K/V matrices from HBM twice per layer vs flash once; ~15-20% of time spent on memory stalls
bottleneck_breakdown.dataloader: negligible (100K token smoke corpus cached in RAM, num_workers=0 OK here)
bottleneck_breakdown.other: CPU roundtrip for phi_holo MI measurement every 100 eval steps — amortized but spikes GPU idle

sanity_checks.implication: VRAM is 57% full. 43GB free → batch size can 2-3x without OOM.
sanity_checks.grad_ckpt_off: note: train_alm_14b_p4.py does NOT set gradient_checkpointing_enable(). Recompute overhead saved.
sanity_checks.peft_saves_bwd: LoRA backprop only writes gradients for 50M params (0.34%), but still needs full-graph forward activations through 14.8B frozen weights. 6N model is still accurate for compute.

top_3_levers.name: Enable flash_attention_2 (attn_implementation='flash_attention_2' in from_pretrained)
top_3_levers.reason: Qwen2.5-14B at seq=1024 has 48 layers of eager softmax(QK^T)V with O(T^2) HBM reads. Flash-attn collapses to O(T) and raises tokens/sec 1.4-1.6x directly. Proven on H100.
top_3_levers.effort: LOW — 1-line edit + `pip install flash-attn`. Already wired in transformers.
top_3_levers.risk: none (bf16 numerically identical output to eager)
top_3_levers.blocker: R-rule: READ-ONLY on training code this task — deliver as ROI queue for next session

top_3_levers.name: Increase batch size from 1 to 4 (grad_accum 8 -> 2, same effective batch)
top_3_levers.estimated_uplift: +15-25% MFU (better kernel occupancy + amortized launch overhead)
top_3_levers.reason: VRAM is only 57% full. At seq=1024 batch=1, many H100 SMs are under-utilized per forward pass. Going to batch=4 uses ~22GB more activations (well inside 43GB headroom) and gives GEMM kernels longer tensor contractions to amortize kernel launches.
top_3_levers.risk: OOM if combined with flash-attn off + full-seq — watch VRAM

top_3_levers.name: Increase seq_len from 1024 to 2048
top_3_levers.reason: Longer seq lens make attention arithmetic intensity higher (compute-bound region). Combined with flash-attn, this is a free MFU win. 14B corpus has plenty of 2K+ token contexts.
top_3_levers.effort: LOW — CLI flag --seq 2048 (requires flash-attn for O(T) memory scaling)
top_3_levers.risk: needs flash-attn first to avoid OOM from O(T^2) attention memory

composite_estimate.all_three_together: 43.7% -> 65-72% MFU (H100 realistic ceiling for LoRA is ~72-75%)
composite_estimate.ceiling_gap: 56.3% absolute from peak 989 TFLOP/s; recoverable portion ~25-30 percentage points
composite_estimate.physics_wall: After all 3 levers, remaining gap to 989 TFLOP/s is hardware memory bandwidth + LoRA dense-base recompute — true physical ceiling for this setup is ~72% MFU

notes.mfu_convention: 6N FLOP/token (Kaplan) including fwd+bwd. Chinchilla uses 6N; some papers use 8N with activation recompute.
notes.lora_caveat: LoRA reduces trainable params by 294x but does NOT reduce forward/backward compute through frozen base weights — bwd still touches all 14.8B params to compute input-grads for activations. MFU calc is same as full fine-tune.
notes.r5_vs_r4: r4 reported 12.9-18.4 step/min per night session memory. r5 is 35.6 step/min — ~2x faster because r5 dropped gradient_checkpointing (r4 had it on for OOM avoidance). Gradient ckpt off = +40-50% speed at cost of 20GB more VRAM, which is available here.
notes.phi_holo_measurement_cost: phi_holo MI compute transfers tensors to CPU every eval (step%100). During that eval, GPU sits ~200ms idle. Amortized over 100 train steps = 0.2% overhead.

verdict.priority: flash_attention_2 is the single biggest unlock. File as ROI queue for next non-READ-ONLY session.
